title: Authorization, Object Ownership, Properties, Functions, and Tenancy · Runtime Contract: Authz Subject Action Resource
summary: Authenticated users can enumerate another tenant’s records, mass-assign privileged fields, call admin actions through hidden routes, and infer denied objects from response differences. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo authz subject action resource increment in deny-by-default authorization decision matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define subject, action, resource, tenant, properties, relationship, context, policy version, and decision evidence for every protected operation.
Treat bearer access tokens as scoped resource-server credentials under RFC 6750 and BCP 9700 without implementing an authorization server casually.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Authz Subject Action Resource · Theory Authz Subject Action Resource
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 2214 in a canary service revision draining long-lived streams. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce tenant, action, and property authorization as resource policy; then cancel during the dependency phase and preserve cleanup plus response ownership. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Authz Subject Action Resource contract for the supplied data.
